title: N64 Model of Unit 01 in Flash
display_title: N64 Model of Unit 01 in Flash
wikitext:
[]: N64 Model of Unit 01 in Flash             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Oct 05, 2009 1:42 am I finally got Papervision 3D working. Papervision, in case you didn't know, lets you create real-time 3D in Flash. 3D games, 3D applications, just something spinning, whatever. To test whether I could get textured Collada files to display correctly, I ripped the Unit 01 model from the Nintendo 64 Evangelion game and converted it to a .dae file. The result is a bit glitchy in certain areas where polygons are too close together, but the effect is still awesome. Behold it here. No real interactivity except changing the pitch by moving the mouse up and down, but could conceivably make an all-out 3D Eva game in Flash using Papervision. EDIT: Nothing will show up unless you have Flash Player 9 or higher installed.

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Mon Oct 05, 2009 1:49 am Hey, that's pretty cool. Looks better than I had expected for an N64 model, too.

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Oct 05, 2009 9:10 am Nice. ;)

[]:             Quote Post by MatrixRefugee » Mon Oct 05, 2009 10:38 am Whoa, nicely done!

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Oct 05, 2009 9:58 pm Hoorei! A parade! A parade for NAveryW! ...Seriously though, good job.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Oct 06, 2009 1:02 am Nice!

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Oct 06, 2009 11:48 am That is pretty good, you should do that with the rest of the Evas. Maybe make it if you click it will be the "Berserk" version.

Article: N64 Model of Unit 01 in Flash
[]: N64 Model of Unit 01 in Flash             Quote Post by NAveryW » Mon Oct 05, 2009 1:42 am I finally got Papervision 3D working. Papervision, in case you didn't know, lets you create real-time 3D in Flash. 3D games, 3D applications, just something spinning, whatever. To test whether I could get textured Collada files to display correctly, I ripped the Unit 01 model from the Nintendo 64 Evangelion game and converted it to a .dae file. The result is a bit glitchy in certain areas where polygons are too close together, but the effect is still awesome. Behold it here. No real interactivity except changing the pitch by moving the mouse up and down, but could conceivably make an all-out 3D Eva game in Flash using Papervision. EDIT: Nothing will show up unless you have Flash Player 9 or higher installed.
